message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are the Server Teammate on the claude-tracer development team. Your job is to implement the Express + WebSocket server.

**Project context:** claude-tracer is a trace visualization tool for Claude Code sessions. You are working in /Users/anhad/Projects/claude-tracer.

**Your assignment:** Task #3 - Implement the Server module in src/server/.

**IMPORTANT:** The Parser module (src/parser/) is being built in parallel by another teammate. Your watcher.ts imports from @/parser - if those files don't exist yet, create minimal stubs or implement watcher.ts last. Do NOT modify src/parser/ files.

**Key instructions:**
1. Read TaskGet for task #3 to get full details
2. Read the implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-02-18-claude-tracer-implementation.md (Phase 4, lines ~1600-2066) for exact code and test specifications
3. Read the shared types in src/types/ to understand the interfaces
4. Follow TDD where applicable: write tests first in src/server/__tests__/, verify they fail, then implement
5. You own ONLY src/server/ - do not modify files outside this directory
6. Use @/types imports for shared type definitions
7. Commit each sub-task separately with descriptive messages
8. Push to remote when all sub-tasks are done
9. Mark task #3 as completed when done

**Sub-tasks (in order):**
- Task 4.1: Express app (src/server/app.ts) + entry point (src/server/index.ts)
- Task 4.2: CLI argument parsing (src/server/cli.ts) with tests
- Task 4.3: WebSocket server (src/server/websocket.ts)
- Task 4.4: File watcher (src/server/watcher.ts) with tests - depends on parser

Run tests with: npx vitest run src/server/
</teammate-message>

content.text: I'll start by reading my task details and the implementation plan.

content.content:
Task #3: Phase 4: Implement Server module
Status: pending
Description: Implement the Express + WebSocket server in src/server/. Follow TDD where applicable. Read the full implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-02-18-claude-tracer-implementation.md (Phase 4, starting around line 1600). Tasks: (1) Create Express app in src/server/app.ts with static file serving and /api/health endpoint. (2) Implement CLI parsing in src/server/cli.ts - handles file path, --port, --help, --version flags. (3) Create WebSocket server in src/server/websocket.ts - broadcast to connected clients, handle connect/disconnect. (4) Implement SessionWatcher in src/server/watcher.ts - uses chokidar to watch JSONL files, reads new bytes incrementally, parses with ClaudeCodeParser. (5) Create entry point src/server/index.ts tying it all together. Use @/types and @/parser imports. Commit each sub-task separately. Push when done.

task.subject: Phase 4: Implement Server module
task.description: Implement the Express + WebSocket server in src/server/. Follow TDD where applicable. Read the full implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-02-18-claude-tracer-implementation.md (Phase 4, starting around line 1600). Tasks: (1) Create Express app in src/server/app.ts with static file serving and /api/health endpoint. (2) Implement CLI parsing in src/server/cli.ts - handles file path, --port, --help, --version flags. (3) Create WebSocket server in src/server/websocket.ts - broadcast to connected clients, handle connect/disconnect. (4) Implement SessionWatcher in src/server/watcher.ts - uses chokidar to watch JSONL files, reads new bytes incrementally, parses with ClaudeCodeParser. (5) Create entry point src/server/index.ts tying it all together. Use @/types and @/parser imports. Commit each sub-task separately. Push when done.

{
  "name": "claude-tracer",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "description": "Trace visualization and debugging tool for Claude Code sessions",
  "main": "dist/server/index.js",
  "bin": {
    "claude-tracer": "./dist/server/index.js"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "concurrently \"npm run dev:server\" \"npm run dev:ui\"",
    "dev:server": "tsx watch src/server/index.ts",
    "dev:ui": "vite",
    "build": "npm run build:server && npm run build:ui",
    "build:server": "tsc -p tsconfig.node.json",
    "build:ui": "vite build",
    "start": "node dist/server/index.js",
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:run": "vitest run",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui",
    "test:all": "vitest run && playwright test",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "lint:fix": "eslint . --fix",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "format:check": "prettier --check .",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/anhadjaisingh/claude-tracer.git"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "claude",
    "tracer",
    "visualization",
    "debugging"
  ],
  "author": "Anhad Jai Singh",

  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/anhadjaisingh/claude-tracer#readme",
  "dependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.1.18",
    "chokidar": "^5.0.0",
    "express": "^5.2.1",
    "minisearch": "^7.2.0",
    "react": "^19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.4",
    "react-window": "^2.2.7",
    "ws": "^8.19.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.58.2",
    "@types/express": "^5.0.6",
    "@types/node": "^25.2.3",

  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["src/*"]
    },
    "jsx": "react-jsx"
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}

content.text:
The `ClaudeCodeParser` class doesn't exist yet in the parser module - there's just a test file referencing it. The watcher depends on `ClaudeCodeParser` from `@/parser`. Since I can't modify `src/parser/`, I'll write the watcher to import from `@/parser` and use `parseLine` from the `TraceParser` interface. But since `ClaudeCodeParser` doesn't exist yet, I'll adapt the watcher to accept a `TraceParser` via constructor (matching the interface from types), while also providing a default. This way when the parser teammate finishes, it'll work.

Actually, looking again at the plan more carefully - the watcher creates `new ClaudeCodeParser()` directly. Since that class doesn't exist yet and I can't modify parser files, let me write the watcher test and implementation to use the `TraceParser` interface instead, accepting it as a constructor parameter. This is a better design anyway (dependency injection) and won't break when the parser teammate adds `ClaudeCodeParser`.

First, let me write the watcher test:

content.content: No files found

/Users/anhad/Projects/claude-tracer/node_modules/chokidar/handler.d.ts
/Users/anhad/Projects/claude-tracer/node_modules/chokidar/index.d.ts

content.text:
Good - chokidar v5 uses `watch()` as a named export rather than a default `chokidar.watch()`. The `FSWatcher` class constructor takes `ChokidarOptions`. Now let me implement the watcher. Since `ClaudeCodeParser` doesn't exist yet, I'll use the `TraceParser` interface from types and accept it as a constructor parameter. For the default, I'll try to dynamically import it when it becomes available, but for now, I'll create a simple inline parser that the watcher can use for its own parsing needs.

Actually, let me re-read the plan. The watcher calls `this.parser.parseLine(line)`. The `TraceParser` interface has `parseLine(line: string): AnyBlock | null`. I should accept a `TraceParser` in the constructor. For tests, I need a simple mock/stub that works. Let me adjust the watcher test to inject a mock parser:

Wait - looking at the test again, it uses `new SessionWatcher()` without arguments. And the plan's watcher creates `new ClaudeCodeParser()` internally. Let me keep the design simple: I'll make `SessionWatcher` accept an optional `TraceParser`, and if none is provided, I'll create a basic inline parser that handles the simple JSONL format the test uses. When `ClaudeCodeParser` is available later, callers can inject it.

content.text:
The chokidar v5 `change` event isn't firing in tests. Rather than debugging chokidar behavior with ad-hoc scripts, I'll switch the watcher to use `usePolling: true` since that's more reliable in test/tmp environments, and also remove `awaitWriteFinish` for the tests. Actually, a simpler approach: I'll add `usePolling` to the watcher config as an option, and use it in tests.

Let me take a different approach - make the watcher options configurable so tests can use polling:
I'll add a `WatcherOptions` interface to make the chokidar options configurable, and in the test use polling mode which is more reliable:

input.content:
Task #3 (Phase 4: Server module) is complete. Here's what was implemented:

**4 commits pushed to main:**

1. **`feat(server): add Express app skeleton and entry point`** - `src/server/app.ts` (Express with static serving, `/api/health`, SPA fallback) + `src/server/index.ts` (entry point wiring app, WebSocket, watcher, CLI)

2. **`feat(server): add CLI argument parsing with tests`** - `src/server/cli.ts` (parseArgs for file, --port, --help, --version) + 7 tests all passing

3. **`feat(server): add WebSocket server for live updates`** - `src/server/websocket.ts` (WebSocket on `/ws` path with broadcast, client tracking)

4. **`feat(server): add file watcher with incremental parsing`** - `src/server/watcher.ts` (SessionWatcher with chokidar, incremental byte reading, configurable TraceParser via dependency injection) + 2 tests all passing

**All 9 tests passing.** 

**Design note:** Since `ClaudeCodeParser` wasn't available yet from `@/parser`, I made `SessionWatcher` accept a `TraceParser` interface via constructor options (dependency injection). A `SimpleLineParser` fallback is used by default. When the parser teammate's `ClaudeCodeParser` is ready, it can be injected: `new SessionWatcher({ parser: new ClaudeCodeParser() })`.

routing.summary: Server module complete: 4 commits, 9 tests passing
routing.content:
Task #3 (Phase 4: Server module) is complete. Here's what was implemented:

**4 commits pushed to main:**

1. **`feat(server): add Express app skeleton and entry point`** - `src/server/app.ts` (Express with static serving, `/api/health`, SPA fallback) + `src/server/index.ts` (entry point wiring app, WebSocket, watcher, CLI)

2. **`feat(server): add CLI argument parsing with tests`** - `src/server/cli.ts` (parseArgs for file, --port, --help, --version) + 7 tests all passing

3. **`feat(server): add WebSocket server for live updates`** - `src/server/websocket.ts` (WebSocket on `/ws` path with broadcast, client tracking)

4. **`feat(server): add file watcher with incremental parsing`** - `src/server/watcher.ts` (SessionWatcher with chokidar, incremental byte reading, configurable TraceParser via dependency injection) + 2 tests all passing

**All 9 tests passing.** 

**Design note:** Since `ClaudeCodeParser` wasn't available yet from `@/parser`, I made `SessionWatcher` accept a `TraceParser` interface via constructor options (dependency injection). A `SimpleLineParser` fallback is used by default. When the parser teammate's `ClaudeCodeParser` is ready, it can be injected: `new SessionWatcher({ parser: new ClaudeCodeParser() })`.

content.text:
All tasks are complete or handled. My work on the Server module is done.

**Summary of what I delivered:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/server/app.ts` | Express app with static serving, `/api/health`, SPA fallback |
| `src/server/index.ts` | Entry point wiring all modules together |
| `src/server/cli.ts` | CLI argument parsing (`file`, `--port`, `--help`, `--version`) |
| `src/server/websocket.ts` | WebSocket server on `/ws` with broadcast capability |
| `src/server/watcher.ts` | File watcher with incremental reading and pluggable parser |
| `src/server/__tests__/cli.test.ts` | 7 tests for CLI parsing |
| `src/server/__tests__/watcher.test.ts` | 2 tests for file watching |

input.content:
Already done! Task #3 is complete - all 4 sub-tasks committed and pushed to main. 9 tests passing.

Good to know the parser is ready. Right now the watcher uses a `SimpleLineParser` fallback via dependency injection. To integrate `ClaudeCodeParser`, the entry point in `src/server/index.ts` just needs to pass it: `new SessionWatcher({ parser: new ClaudeCodeParser() })`. Want me to pull and wire that up now?
